svg.title: UAC-0057 / Ghostwriter OYSTERFRESH Prometheus kill chain (May 2026)
svg.desc: Two-lane vertical kill chain. Left lane traces the victim Windows endpoint at a Ukrainian government organisation: spearphishing email from a compromised gov mailbox; PDF attachment linking to a ZIP that contains an OYSTERFRESH JavaScript loader; wscript.exe executes OYSTERFRESH which applies a stacked string-reversal plus ROT13 plus URL-decoding chain; OYSTERBLUES is written as an encrypted blob into HKCU registry; OYSTERSHUCK decodes it and establishes persistence via a Microsoft Edge Update masquerade Run key and a scheduled task; on operator validation, a Cobalt Strike beacon is delivered via JavaScript eval(). Right lane shows attacker infrastructure: compromised Ukrainian government mailbox as sender pivot; Cloudflare-fronted .icu domain set serving both the ZIP delivery and the C2 channel; operator-triage tier reading the ~10-minute host profile heartbeat; Cobalt Strike team server delivering the post-exploitation beacon. Footer maps each Sigma, KQL, YARA and Suricata rule plus each PEAK hunt to the stage it instruments. Source: CERT-UA advisory 6315762 (22-May-2026) plus ESET FrostyNeighbor PicassoLoader companion campaign report (14-May-2026).
svg.text: UAC-0057 / Ghostwriter — OYSTERFRESH JavaScript loader + OYSTERBLUES registry persistence
CERT-UA advisory 6315762 (22-May-2026) + ESET FrostyNeighbor PicassoLoader companion (14-May-2026) · Belarus-aligned (UNC1151 baseline Mandiant 2021) · victims: Ukrainian gov + defense
Detection-diary Day 28 — 2026-05-25 · Author: Jarmi
Victim host (Ukrainian government endpoint)
Attacker infrastructure (UAC-0057)
Spearphishing email from compromised gov mailbox
SPF/DKIM/DMARC pass — sender is a previously compromised
Ukrainian government mailbox (UNC1151 feeder pipeline,
CERT Polska Roundcube CVE-2024-42009 mailbox-theft Q3 2025).
From: <gov-tenant> Attachment: prometheus_certificate.pdf
PDF body contains external link to a ZIP archive download.
PDF link → ZIP archive → OYSTERFRESH .js
Victim clicks the PDF link, downloads the ZIP, extracts
it under %TEMP% or Downloads and double-clicks the .js.
Drop: %TEMP%\<random>.js (OYSTERFRESH loader)
Companion chain (ESET): RAR + PicassoLoader, same operator.
MITRE: T1204.002 + T1480 (Ukraine geofence on delivery)
wscript.exe runs OYSTERFRESH — stacked decode chain
Windows Script Host executes the .js; decoy document shown.
Decode chain: string reversal + ROT13 + URL-decoding.
explorer.exe -> wscript.exe "<temp>\<name>.js"
JS uses WScript.Shell + ActiveXObject for next-stage write.
OYSTERBLUES — encrypted blob written to HKCU registry
No PE on disk for the persisted second stage. The encrypted
payload lives inside an HKCU\Software registry value, retrieved
and decrypted in memory by OYSTERSHUCK at runtime.
HKCU\Software\<path> REG_BINARY / large REG_SZ blob
Hunt anchor: abnormally large HKCU\Software value size.
OYSTERSHUCK decodes + persistence (Edge Update masquerade)
Two anchors for survivability — Run key plus scheduled task.
Genuine Edge tasks suffix Core / UA; attacker omits suffix.
HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\
MicrosoftEdgeUpdate → %APPDATA%\MicrosoftEdgeUpdate.exe
EdgeApp → %APPDATA%\EdgeApp.exe
Task: MicrosoftEdgeUpdateTaskMachine Action: wscript.exe
Cobalt Strike beacon — delivered via JavaScript eval()
After operator validates the ~10-minute host profile heartbeat,
the C2 returns a JS blob the implant passes through eval().
The blob loads the CSBEACON DLL — standard Cobalt Strike
post-exploitation tradecraft from this point onward.
eval(blob_decoded) → CSBEACON DLL load
Beacon: named pipe \\.\pipe\msagent_<hex>, sleep mask,
malleable C2 profile, follow-on Rubeus / Mimikatz / SMB lateral.
Discovery: COMPUTERNAME / USER / OS / LastBoot / proc list
Compromised Ukrainian gov mailbox (sender pivot)
Long-running UNC1151 initial-access primitive — credential
theft via Roundcube XSS (CVE-2024-42009, CERT Polska 2025-06)
supplies the mailbox pool. The cluster scrapes the contact
list and propagates phishing through the compromised user.
SPF/DKIM/DMARC pass for the gov tenant.
Sender reputation defenses are not effective here.
Detection point: link + attachment semantic, not domain rep.
.icu domain set behind Cloudflare CDN — delivery + C2
Single infrastructure cluster serves both the lure-side ZIP
download and the OYSTERBLUES POST C2. UAC-0057 has used .icu
heavily across multiple campaigns since 2022 — the TLD itself
is statistically rare in legitimate Ukrainian gov outbound.
DNS: *.icu (rotating FQDNs, CF-fronted)
TLS: SNI in .icu, certs are Cloudflare-issued
HTTP: POST /api/<ep> with JSON system-profile body
UA: Mozilla/5.0 ... (script-host fingerprints also seen)
Cadence ~10 minutes — the heartbeat that supplies the operator
with the steady stream needed for the go/no-go decision on
the Cobalt Strike payload.
Cloudflare front masks the origin IP across rotations.
Defense: anchor on .icu TLD + cadence + Cloudflare ASN combo;
FQDN rotates faster than detection-content cycles.
References: CERT-UA 6315762 (current FQDN set in advisory)
Operator triage — manual go/no-go on every victim
UAC-0057 does not auto-deploy Cobalt Strike on first beacon.
The operator reads the ~10-minute host profile, checks the
user / domain / OS / process list, and only then returns the
CSBEACON-loader eval() JS to interesting victims.
Sandbox attempts from non-Ukraine egress IPs are filtered
at the delivery stage (geofencing in companion PicassoLoader
chain confirmed by ESET 2026-05-14). Detonate from a
Ukrainian-IP sandbox or detonate offline to see the full chain.
MITRE: (operator-side, no ATT&CK tag)
Cobalt Strike team server — post-exploitation tier
Standard Cobalt Strike tradecraft — sleep mask, named pipe
internal pivot, malleable C2 profile leftovers, sometimes
Rubeus / Mimikatz / SMBExec follow-on. Network anchors:
Beacon URI pattern (per malleable C2 profile)
Named pipe \\.\pipe\msagent_<hex>
Sleep mask + jitter
Beacon-stager DNS A-record or TLS SNI to CS infrastructure
Hunt for CSBEACON DLL load + named-pipe creation on the
same host that exhibited the OYSTERBLUES POST cadence.
Detection anchors (this folder)
Sigma ×3: wscript+js from archive (stage 3) · HKCU Run-key Edge masquerade (stage 5) · Scheduled-task Edge masquerade (stage 5)
KQL ×3: wscript+js + .icu POST correlation (stages 3-8) · Run-key Edge masquerade hunt (stage 5) · .icu DNS + POST correlation (stage 8)
YARA ×1 (uac0057_oysterfresh_js_loader, stage 3) · Suricata 1 file 5 sids (.icu DNS/SNI/host/POST/UA, stages 3+8) · PEAK hunts ×3 (H1 stage 3, H2 stage 5, H3 stage 8)
IOCs (iocs.csv): family-name strings, persistence regkeys, persistence paths, .icu TLD anchor, decoder primitives, CVE-2024-42009 feeder, prior-campaign references
